reference: S. Lauritzen, D. Spiegelhalter (1988). "Local Computation with Probabilities on Graphical Structures and their Application to Expert Systems (with discussion)." Journal of the Royal Statistical Society: Series B, 50(2):157–224.
context: Small synthetic dataset about lung diseases (tuberculosis, lung cancer, bronchitis) and shortness of breath (dyspnoea). A recent visit to Asia increases the chance of tuberculosis, while smoking is a risk factor for lung cancer and bronchitis. Chest X-ray and dyspnoea do not discriminate well between lung cancer and tuberculosis in this toy model.

variables.name: dysp
variables.description: Dyspnoea (shortness of breath); whether the patient has dyspnoea (levels: yes, no).

variables.name: tub
variables.description: Tuberculosis; whether the patient has tuberculosis (levels: yes, no).

variables.name: lung
variables.description: Lung cancer; whether the patient has lung cancer (levels: yes, no).

variables.name: bronc
variables.description: Bronchitis; whether the patient has bronchitis (levels: yes, no).

variables.name: asia
variables.description: Visit to Asia; whether the patient has recently visited Asia (levels: yes, no).

variables.name: smoke
variables.description: Smoking; whether the patient smokes (levels: yes, no).

variables.name: xray
variables.description: Chest X-ray; whether the chest X-ray result is positive/abnormal (levels: yes, no).

variables.name: either
variables.description: Tuberculosis versus lung cancer/bronchitis; an intermediate binary indicator used in the model (levels: yes, no).
